channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Swastika graffiti, 7th June
item.description:
Not sure who is responsible but this is on a well used footpath and having Heil Hitler and a swastika isn’t appropriate. On side of substation building.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 158 - 162, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I have an electric vehicle. I have to remove these line bikes on a daily basis ASB from the EV charging point,bay. These line bikes are being discarded in selfish manner damaging people's car, property on a daily basis. I am a resident & had to move over 20 line bikes out of the EV charging point just to use it because the bikes were left in the wrong bay., 7th June
item.description:
I have an electric vehicle. I have to remove these line bikes on a daily basis ASB from the EV charging point,bay. These line bikes are being discarded in selfish manner damaging people's car, property on a daily basis. I am a resident & had to move over 2

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on fence next to footway, 7th June
item.description:
Graffiti on fence next to footway connecting to Gipsy Hill with end of Becondale Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Becondale Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big tree, 7th June
item.description:
I bought this property 7 years ago. I was told by the seller that I am responsible for the tree . But I’m Not 100% sure if I am as it’s very near to the public pavement . I want to trim it or cut it down in the future. I would like to know if the tree is legally owned by me or if the tree has an ant preservation orders?Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shelley Drive, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Line bike causing anti-social behavior and nuisance to members are public and residents on a daily basis after moving these bikes out the way or off of people's cars. Hackney Council only care about investment and money that they are receiving from line bikes. Line bikes are a inconvenience for many people causing upset and obstructions in the areaYes, Hackney Council receives funds from a new agreement with e-bike operators Lime and Voi, from which it will receive a cut of the income. The council also partners with other organizations and initiatives to provide and fund various hire schemes, such as the "Cargo Bike Share" scheme, which is part of a wider network of shared bikes, and has secured funding through grants from the government for a "Cycle Loan" program for its employees., 7th June
item.description:
Can the new mayor of Hackney please address this issue of abandoned line bites causing obstruction in public parking base pedestrian footpath disabled people struggling to use curve because bikes blocking pathway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Semley Gate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
